governing_requirement: Every bridge failure maps deterministically to a closed category, retryability, exact affected scope, completion-profile impact, remediation, permitted lifecycle transition, and no-fallback reason; contradictions and unknowns fail closed.
prerequisites: MF-P6-09.01 through MF-P6-09.05 remain open or dependency-held; implementation must consume their normalized identity, transform, artifact, authority and eligibility decisions after adoption.

existing_enforcement.frozen_wire_schema: mask_bridge_error.schema.json is closed and requires code, category, retryable, impact_scope, affected_scope, remediation, no_silent_fallback, message and details hash.
existing_enforcement.unknown_code_behavior: Frozen schema rejects an unknown code with enum validation.

confirmed_gaps.probe: Create policy-consistent SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, CERTIFICATE_REVOKED, ARTIFACT_HASH_MISMATCH, TRANSFORM_MISMATCH and INVARIANT_VIOLATION documents with every affected_scope array empty.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Each code/domain must require the exact relevant correlation, pass, release, capability, artifact, certificate, provider, ontology, policy, owner, transform or run scope and reject missing or extraneous scope.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The frozen v1 error record contains no required/optional profile impact decision.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: An additive decision must state core_autonomous_runtime, independent_real_accuracy and scale_daz_maturity impact without allowing optional profile failure to fabricate core failure.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The current matrix does not fix the only permitted durable transition for each code.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Fix transitions such as retry_wait, reconcile_unknown, blocked_dependency, quarantine, invalidate, revalidate, rollback or terminal_fail and reject contradictions.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The wire has no_silent_fallback=true but no closed reason explaining why fallback is forbidden for the exact domain.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Derive a closed no-fallback reason from code/category/scope rather than accepting free-form provider text.

confirmed_gaps.observed: Capability maps into compatibility, transform maps into geometry, trust/integrity map into security, and invariant maps into internal; there is no explicit normalized domain covering all required tracker categories.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: The additive decision must expose the exact normalized domain while preserving the frozen v1 category as an input observation.

confirmed_gaps.observed: The in-code tuple matrix is not a versioned self-hashed policy and produces validation issues, not one canonical decision record binding normalized context and deterministic output.
confirmed_gaps.required_correction: Add a self-hashed policy, normalized input facts, canonical decision and golden vectors.

implementation_packet.objective: Implement a producer-owned additive bridge-error decision around the frozen v1 error record and current 37-code matrix, after upstream normalized decisions are adopted.
implementation_packet.path_state: All five allowed paths are absent and clean. Candidate files are read-only. Allowed paths are disjoint from frozen/queued 08.05, 08.06, 08.07 and 09.01 plus prepared 09.02, 09.04 and 09.05 paths.
acceptance_criteria: Load one closed versioned self-hashed policy whose code set exactly equals the frozen v1 schema enum and existing BRIDGE_ERROR_POLICY set; missing, extra, duplicate or drifted codes fail closed.

Map every code to exact normalized domain, frozen category, retryability, impact scope, required/nonempty affected-scope keys, completion-profile impact, remediation, delay/runbook/reroute constraints, permitted lifecycle transition and closed no-fallback reason.

Derive affected scope from normalized request, identity, transform, artifact, authority, revocation, release, capability and runtime facts; caller-supplied empty, extraneous or contradictory scope cannot pass.
Preserve optional-profile versus core impact so independent accuracy or DAZ maturity failure cannot redefine core autonomous completion.
Unknown error codes, unknown policy versions, incomplete context and policy/wire disagreement yield a deterministic nonretryable fail-closed decision with no fallback.
Emit policy ID/hash, frozen error hash, normalized facts, exact decision fields, closed ordered reasons and canonical decision hash under a strict schema.
Golden vectors cover all 37 codes, every required domain, exact scope, contradiction, unknown code, policy drift, optional/core profile separation, and record-order determinism.
No decision silently substitutes an empty mask, different person, weaker authority, unqualified provider, stale cache or unrelated release; unrelated scopes remain unaffected.
Frozen v1 schema, current validation.py matrix, fixtures, catalog, semantic profile and all held worker paths remain byte-identical.

claim_boundary: This audit proves the remaining additive decision gap and preserves the stronger existing 37-code matrix. It does not implement the decision, complete MF-P6-09.06, or claim error recovery, retries, lifecycle, release, cache, adapter or end-to-end runtime completion.
